# AgentPassport — Agent Skill

> Real-world infrastructure for AI agents. Today: email inboxes on a domain
> the user owns. Coming: residential IP proxies and VPN tunnels by country.

One CLI, one SDK, one API key. NOT passport.js. NOT an AI-identity protocol.
NOT temp-mail — every address is bound to a domain the user owns.

## Install

```
npm install -g @agentpassportsh/cli       # CLI
npm install @agentpassportsh/sdk          # SDK
```

## Authenticate

```
export AP_API_KEY=your_agentpassport_api_key
```

Get a key at https://agentpassport.sh.

## Capability status

- Email — receive on a domain the user owns — shipped
- Email — send from a domain the user owns — shipped
- Domains — shipped (precondition for email)
- Proxy — coming (residential rotating IPs by country/city)
- VPN — coming (WireGuard tunnels by region)

## Domain prerequisite (human, once per domain)

```
app domain add myagent.com
# returns 2 nameservers; user sets them at their registrar.
# 5–30 min propagation.
app domain status myagent.com --wait
```

## Email — common tasks (agent-runnable)

- Provision an inbox:
  `app email create --domain myagent.com --name support`
- Wait for the next message (msg.raw has the full RFC 5322):
  `app email watch --inbox support@myagent.com --timeout 60s --json`
- Read latest N messages (newest first):
  `app email read --inbox support@myagent.com -n 10 --json`
- Filter by substring on the raw message:
  `app email read --inbox support@myagent.com --filter "verification" --json`
- Send mail from any address on a domain the user owns (no inbox
  needs to exist for `from`; domain ownership is the only check):
  `app email send --from noreply@myagent.com --to b@y.com --subject "..." --body "..."`
  SDK: `await ap.email.send({ from, to, subject, text, html?, replyTo? })`

Every inbound message has shape:

```ts
{ id, to, receivedAt, sentAt, from, subject, text, html, raw }
```

`from / subject / text / html` are parsed server-side from the standard
RFC 5322 fields (no OTP-style heuristics — just spec-defined header /
MIME extraction). `raw` is the full original message for edge cases.

Pull a code:

```
code=$(app email watch --inbox $INBOX --timeout 60s --json \
        | head -1 | jq -r .text | grep -oE '\b[0-9]{4,8}\b' | head -1)
```

TypeScript:

```ts
for await (const msg of ap.email.watch({ inbox, timeoutMs: 60_000 })) {
  const code = (msg.text ?? "").match(/\b\d{4,8}\b/)?.[0];
  if (code) { await externalService.verify({ email: inbox, code }); break; }
}
```
